messages.content: please tell me which mouses can be used on glass?

Some of the mouses that can be used on glass include:

1. Logitech MX Anywhere 2S Wireless Mouse
2. Microsoft Arc Touch Mouse
3. HP x4000b Bluetooth Mouse
4. J-Tech Digital M998 Wireless Vertical Mouse
5. Microsoft Surface Arc Mouse
6. VicTsing Wireless Mouse
7. Jelly Comb 2.4G Slim Wireless Mouse
8. AmazonBasics Wireless Mouse with Nano Receiver
9. TeckNet Pro 2.4G Ergonomic Wireless Mouse
10. Anker Wireless Mouse
Are there any sony mouse can do it?

Sony doesn't currently have any mouses that are specifically designed to work on glass surfaces. However, some users have reported that the Sony VAIO Bluetooth Laser Mouse works on some types of glass surfaces. It is important to note that using any mouse on glass surfaces can be unpredictable and that some types of glass may not work with any mouse accurately.
Please tell me about Tannoy prestige speakers?

Tannoy is a UK-based loudspeaker manufacturer that has been producing speakers for over 90 years. The Tannoy Prestige series is one of the company's high-end lines of speakers. The Prestige range features a series of high-quality speakers that are designed to deliver incredibly clean, accurate sound with an unprecedented level of detail.

The Prestige series consists of the following speakers:

1. Canterbury: The Canterbury is a floorstanding speaker that features a 15" Dual Concentric driver and a 15" bass driver. It is designed to provide deep, powerful bass and incredibly accurate midrange and higher frequencies.

2. Kensington: The Kensington is a smaller floorstanding speaker that features a 12" Dual Concentric driver and a 12" bass driver. It is designed to deliver a more compact sound with impressive bass response and excellent clarity.

3. Turnberry: The Turnberry is a floorstanding speaker that features a 10" Dual Concentric driver and a 10" bass driver. It is designed to provide a more focused, detailed sound that is ideal for smaller rooms.

4. Stirling: The Stirling is a bookshelf speaker that features an 8" Dual Concentric driver and an 8" bass driver. It is designed to be compact and versatile, and it can be used in a wide range of environments.

Overall, the Tannoy Prestige series speakers are highly regarded for their incredibly detailed and accurate sound and their exceptional build quality. They are ideal for audiophiles who demand the highest level of sound quality from their speakers.
Are they all made in UK?

Tannoy is a British company, and all of its speaker designs and engineering take place in the UK. However, not all Tannoy speakers are currently manufactured in the UK. The company has manufacturing plants in China and Europe as well, which produce some of the Tannoy speaker lines, including the Prestige series. 

That being said, Tannoy takes great care to ensure that the build quality and sound quality of all their speakers meet the high standards of the brand and its loyal fans.
It means that Stirling and Turnberry maybe  made in China?

Yes, it's possible that the Stirling and Turnberry models in the Tannoy Prestige series are manufactured in China or Europe, depending on the specific model and production batch. However, as previously mentioned, Tannoy maintains strict quality control measures and works closely with its manufacturing partners to ensure the sound and build quality meets the high standards of the brand.
I have heard the in the past years , Tannoy has made some speakers in Japan?

While Tannoy has always had its manufacturing base in the UK, the company has occasionally partnered with other manufacturers to produce some of its speaker models in Japan. For example, in the late 1970s, Tannoy partnered with a company called ALR Jordan to produce some of its speakers in Japan. However, this arrangement was relatively short-lived, and Tannoy has not pursued any other significant manufacturing partnerships outside of its primary UK production facilities. 

That being said, it's worth noting that the majority of Tannoy speakers, including the Prestige series, are designed and engineered in the UK, regardless of where they are ultimately manufactured.
Do you know about a amplifier brand AURA, that connected with B&W?

I am not familiar with an amplifier brand called AURA. It's possible that the brand may be relatively new, or not well-known in the audio industry. 

However, Bowers & Wilkins (B&W) is a well-respected speaker brand that produces a wide range of high-quality speakers across various price points. B&W speakers have been known to work well with a variety of amplifiers from different brands, so it's possible that the AURA amplifier could work with B&W speakers as well.

That being said, it's important to ensure that the amplifier is matched appropriately to the specific B&W speaker model you have. Speaker and amplifier compatibility can depend on a variety of factors, including impedance, power handling, and sensitivity. It's always a good idea to consult with an audio professional or the manufacturer to ensure that the amplifier you choose is the right match for your B&W speakers.
If I have a pair of B&W matrix 803s2 speakers, which amplifier is the best to it?

The Bowers & Wilkins Matrix 803 Series 2 speakers are excellent speakers that require an amplifier with enough power to drive them well. Here are some suitable amplifier options for your speakers:

1. Cambridge Audio CXA81: This Class AB integrated amplifier delivers 80 watts per channel at 8 ohms and uses a high-quality DAC to provide excellent detail and clarity.

2. NAD C 368: This integrated amplifier delivers 80 watts per channel at 8 ohms and uses NAD's renowned HybridDigital technology to deliver superb sound quality.

3. Yamaha A-S1200: This Class A/B integrated amplifier delivers 90 watts per channel at 8 ohms and features a retro-inspired design, along with Yamaha's proprietary ToP-ART circuitry for superior sound quality.

4. Rotel RA-1572: This Class AB integrated amplifier delivers 120 watts per channel at 8 ohms and features a high-end AKM digital-to-analog converter for exceptional sound quality.

5. Anthem STR Integrated Amplifier: This hybrid analog/digital integrated amplifier delivers 200 watts per channel at 4 ohms and features Anthem's proprietary ARC Genesis Room Correction technology for optimal sound quality.

These amplifier options are just a few examples of the many suitable options available for the B&W Matrix 803 Series 2 speakers. The important thing is to choose an amplifier that can deliver enough power to properly drive your speakers and that is compatible with your other audio components and preferences.

The Bowers & Wilkins (B&W) Matrix 803 Series 2 speaker is rated at 200 watts maximum power handling. However, it's important to note that the maximum power rating is not the only factor to consider when choosing an amplifier. Other important factors include the amplifier's impedance, frequency response, sensitivity, and total harmonic distortion (THD). Matching the amplifier's specifications to those of the speaker is essential to achieve the best sound quality and avoid damaging the speaker.
